title: ORCL, ADBE, SNPS, AVGO, COST, CIEN, LULU, TOL, AZO, FERG, CASY, AVAV, SAIL, GME, | Q3 2025 Earnings Preview: December 8-11, 2025
author: tickeron_community
selftext:
# Key Points

* **Semiconductor and software heavyweights** ([ORCL](https://tickeron.com/ticker/ORCL/), [ADBE](https://tickeron.com/ticker/ADBE/), [SNPS](https://tickeron.com/ticker/SNPS/), [AVGO](https://tickeron.com/ticker/AVGO/), [CIEN](https://tickeron.com/ticker/CIEN/)) are expected to showcase robust AI-driven growth, with record backlogs and accelerating data center demand underpinning valuations.
* **Infrastructure and consumer discretionary** ([COST](https://tickeron.com/ticker/COST/), [LULU](https://tickeron.com/ticker/LULU/), [TOL](https://tickeron.com/ticker/TOL/)) will reveal resilience amid inflation and labor cost pressures, with holiday season performance and inventory management in focus.
* **Distribution and retail sector performers** ([AZO](https://tickeron.com/ticker/AZO/), [FERG](https://tickeron.com/ticker/FERG/), [CASY](https://tickeron.com/ticker/CASY/)) demonstrate operational efficiency gains and pricing power, offsetting margin pressures from commodity and wage inflation.
* **Defense and defense-adjacent dynamics** ([AVAV](https://tickeron.com/ticker/AVAV/)) and **identity security** ([SAIL](https://tickeron.com/ticker/SAIL/)) reflect evolving geopolitical and cybersecurity priorities, while **retail turnaround narratives** ([GME](https://tickeron.com/ticker/GME/)) introduce volatility and execution risk into portfolio positioning.
* **Fed rate cut expectations** at approximately 87% probability this week will shape market sentiment for growth equity valuations, particularly benefiting rate-sensitive technology and housing sectors.
